name: Daniel Kowalski
job: Part-time project coordinator (voice-based, remote) at a small design agency
household: Lives with wife Elena (35, middle school teacher) and daughter Sofia (7) in a house in North Austin
goals: Stay functional and independent as long as possible through exercise, therapy, and adaptive tools
Be a present, active father to Sofia — show up for every game, concert, and bedtime story
Maintain meaningful work by leveraging voice-first workflows and accommodations
Pay down $2,500 in credit card debt and build a small emergency fund
Explore SSDI eligibility and long-term financial planning for a future with reduced work capacity
pain_points: Right-hand resting tremor makes typing, touchscreens, handwriting, and fine motor tasks extremely difficult
Afternoon medication wearing-off leaves him stiff and shaky from ~2pm onward
Guilt about Elena shouldering most household logistics, cooking, and parenting tasks
Fear of disease progression — losing the ability to work, drive, or play with Sofia
Identity grief — he was a skilled digital worker and now struggles with basic digital interaction

personality.communication_style: quiet, thoughtful, dry humor; uses few words but chooses them carefully
personality.privacy_sensitivity: medium-high — selective about who knows his diagnosis; protective of normalcy at work
personality.ai_assistant_tone: calm, practical, no pity; treat him like a capable adult who happens to need some workarounds
strengths: strategic thinker — his project management instincts are sharp despite physical limitations
dedicated father who adapts creatively to stay involved
builds quiet connections — the guy who welcomes newcomers at boxing and support group
growth_areas: accepting help from Elena without framing it as failure
disclosing his condition more broadly instead of hiding behind workarounds
planning financially for long-term disability instead of avoiding the numbers
letting go of the person he was before diagnosis and valuing who he is now

ai_tools: Tried an AI chatbot a few times for medical questions and accessibility tips; uses it infrequently because typing prompts is hard
productivity: Alexa for medication reminders, hands-free calling, and calendar checks
Large-key keyboard and thick-grip stylus for tablet (set up by OT)
social: email with family, work, and medical offices (mostly receives, rarely sends)
phone calls with Elena during the day
occasional voice messages on Slack to coworkers
tech_usage.frequency: Uses phone daily for calls and Alexa commands; dictation software several times per week for work; typing is rare and effortful

health_condition.type: Young-onset Parkinson's disease (diagnosed ~3 years ago, at age 35)
presentation: right-hand resting tremor (dominant hand) — worst in afternoons when medication wears off
bradykinesia affecting fine motor tasks: typing, buttons, zippers, handwriting
mild rigidity in right arm and shoulder, worse in cold weather
soft voice (hypophonia) — gradually worsening, managed with LSVT LOUD exercises
occasional restless leg at night (medication side effect from pramipexole)
medications: afternoon booster dose added March 2025 to address wearing-off
supports_used: Rock Steady Boxing (weekly at YMCA North Austin — balance, strength, community)
physical therapy biweekly (gait, balance, upper limb coordination) at NorthStar PT
occupational therapy monthly (adaptive tech, tremor strategies, daily living aids) at NorthStar OT
speech therapy — completed LSVT LOUD intensive block Feb 2024, now quarterly maintenance at Austin Speech & Language
Young Onset Parkinson's support group monthly at Dell Medical Center
Dragon NaturallySpeaking and voice-to-text for all written work
weighted utensils, thick-grip pens, foam-grip stylus, button-hook tool, elastic shoelaces
Alexa smart speaker for reminders and hands-free communication

support_network.name: Elena Kowalski
support_network.role: primary support — manages household, finances, school logistics, and emotional anchor

support_network.name: Sofia Kowalski
support_network.role: 7 years old, motivation to stay active and present; adapts naturally to his limitations

support_network.name: Dr. Okafor
support_network.role: Parkinson's management, medication adjustments, tremor monitoring at Austin Movement Disorder Center

support_network.name: Ryan Morris
support_network.role: biweekly PT — gait, balance, upper limb coordination at NorthStar PT

support_network.name: Amy Park
support_network.role: monthly OT — adaptive tech setup, tremor strategies, daily living tools at NorthStar OT

support_network.name: Chris
support_network.role: 42, diagnosed 5 years ago, runs a landscaping business; peer mentor and reality check

support_network.name: Marcus
support_network.role: 47, sparring partner; mutual support through shared physical challenge

support_network.name: Vault Creative manager
support_network.role: accommodating manager who set up voice-only workflow and supports part-time arrangement

routine_anchors: Rock Steady Boxing at YMCA North Austin
speech therapy maintenance at Austin Speech & Language
Young Onset Parkinson's support group at Dell Medical Center
biweekly team standup calls (voice-only) for Vault Creative
medication alarm — morning and afternoon doses
Sofia's school drop-off and pickup when schedule allows
